title: Scholarship notice
subtitle: Ministry of Social Justice
text:
GOVERNMENT OF INDIA
MINISTRY OF SOCIAL JUSTICE AND EMPOWERMENT

SUBJECT: Post-Matric Scholarship for Students belonging to Scheduled Castes (SC) for studies in India for the year 2024-25.

In pursuance of the provisions contained in Clause (1) of Article 15 and Clause (4) of Article 16 of the Constitution of India, and in supersession of all previous orders on the subject, the Government of India hereby invites applications from eligible candidates belonging to Scheduled Castes for the award of Post-Matric Scholarships.

ELIGIBILITY: The applicant must belong to Scheduled Caste as notified by the Central/State Government. The total annual income of the applicant's parents/guardians from all sources shall not exceed Rs. 2,50,000/- (Rupees Two Lakh Fifty Thousand only). The applicant must have secured admission in a recognized institution/university for post-matriculation or post-secondary course. Renewal of scholarship shall be subject to satisfactory academic progress as certified by the Head of the Institution.

RATE OF SCHOLARSHIP: Maintenance allowance shall be payable at rates specified in the annexure appended hereto. In addition, compulsory non-refundable fees actually charged by the institution shall be reimbursable, subject to ceiling prescribed by the competent authority. Study tour charges, thesis typing/printing charges, and book allowance shall be admissible as per norms.

APPLICATION PROCEDURE: Applications shall be submitted through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) at scholarships.gov.in. The last date for submission of fresh applications is 30th November 2024. Incomplete applications or applications received after the due date shall be summarily rejected without any further correspondence.

Documents required to be uploaded: (i) Caste Certificate issued by competent authority (ii) Income Certificate of parents/guardians (iii) Mark sheet of qualifying examination (iv) Fee receipt of current course (v) Bank account details with IFSC code (vi) Aadhaar number (vii) Passport size photograph.

title: Ration card update
subtitle: District Supply Office
text:
OFFICE OF THE DISTRICT SUPPLY OFFICER
DEPARTMENT OF FOOD, CIVIL SUPPLIES AND CONSUMER AFFAIRS

CIRCULAR NO. DSO/RC/2024/1847

SUBJECT: Mandatory Aadhaar seeding and e-KYC verification of all ration card holders under the National Food Security Act (NFSA), 2013 — reg.

In compliance with the directives issued by the Department of Food & Public Distribution, Government of India vide letter no. F.No.9-1/2023-BP-II dated 15.03.2024, it is hereby notified that all ration card holders under NFSA are required to complete Aadhaar seeding and biometric authentication (e-KYC) of all family members listed in the ration card.

FAILURE TO COMPLY: Ration cards of beneficiaries who fail to complete Aadhaar seeding and e-KYC verification by 31st December 2024 shall be liable for temporary suspension/deactivation. During the period of suspension, the beneficiary shall NOT be entitled to draw subsidized foodgrains from any Fair Price Shop (FPS) under NFSA.

PROCEDURE: Beneficiaries may visit their nearest Common Service Centre (CSC) or designated Fair Price Shop equipped with biometric devices for completing the e-KYC process. Beneficiaries must carry the following original documents: (a) Ration Card (b) Aadhaar Card of all family members (c) Any one photo ID proof. In case of mismatch in demographic details between Aadhaar and Ration Card, beneficiaries must first get corrections done through the Aadhaar Enrolment Centre before proceeding with e-KYC.

EXEMPTIONS: Senior citizens above 80 years, differently-abled persons, and pregnant/lactating women who are unable to visit CSC/FPS may avail doorstep verification facility by contacting the helpline 1967 or submitting a written request at the taluk office.

For any grievances or queries, beneficiaries may contact the District Supply Officer at dso-office@gov.in or call the toll-free helpline 1800-XXX-XXXX during working hours (10:00 AM to 5:00 PM, Monday to Friday).

title: Property tax notice
subtitle: Municipal Corporation
text:
MUNICIPAL CORPORATION OF GREATER MUMBAI
ASSESSMENT AND COLLECTION DEPARTMENT

NOTICE UNDER SECTION 203 READ WITH SECTION 264 OF THE MUMBAI MUNICIPAL CORPORATION ACT, 1888

PROPERTY TAX DEMAND NOTICE FOR THE YEAR 2024-25

To: The Owner/Occupier of Property bearing Assessment No. ___________

Whereas the rateable value of the above-mentioned property has been determined/revised by the Assessment Department in accordance with the provisions of Chapter VIII of the Mumbai Municipal Corporation Act, 1888, and whereas the property tax for the year 2024-25 has been calculated on the basis of the Capital Value System as approved by the Standing Committee vide Resolution No. SC/2023/456.

You are hereby called upon to pay the total property tax amount of Rs. __________ (inclusive of General Tax, Education Cess, Water Benefit Tax, Sewerage Benefit Tax, Road Tax, Tree Tax, and Fire Tax) within 90 (ninety) days from the date of service of this notice.

DEFAULT: In the event of non-payment within the stipulated period, interest at the rate of 2% per month shall be levied on the outstanding amount from the date of default. Furthermore, the Municipal Commissioner is empowered under Section 203 of the Act to initiate recovery proceedings including attachment and sale of movable property and/or distress warrant.

OBJECTION/APPEAL: If you are aggrieved by the assessment, you may file an objection before the Designated Officer within 15 days from receipt of this notice, along with supporting documents including Property Card (7/12 extract or City Survey record), Sale Deed/Agreement, Previous Tax Receipts, Occupation Certificate from BMC, and Index-II from Sub-Registrar Office.

PAYMENT MODE: Payment may be made through (i) Online portal at portal.mcgm.gov.in (ii) Authorized bank branches (iii) Ward Office Collection Counter. Early payment before 30th June 2024 shall entitle the taxpayer to a rebate of 2% on the total demand.
